name: Google Guessing Game Dataset
description: 10 counting problems where every item is individually visible in the image and the exact count is independently verified. Six samples from the FSC-147 few-shot counting benchmark (dot-annotated by researchers); four from the Puzzle a Day blog (author-verified with revealed answers). Built for a hackathon.

examples.question: How many apples are in this picture?
examples.verification: Dot-annotated by FSC-147 authors (Ranjan et al., CVPR 2021). Count = number of point annotations.

examples.question: How many eggs are in this carton?
How many gingerbread people do we have in the picture?
How many mushrooms do we have in the picture?
How many full cupcakes are in this picture?
How many triangles can you find in this figure (including all sizes)?
examples.source_name: Puzzle a Day — 'How Many Triangles Can You Find?' (2023-06-03)
examples.verification: Answer published on the post by the puzzle author.

examples.question: How many coins are in this picture?
How many squares are in this grid (including all sizes)?
examples.verification: Answer published on the post: total of 224 squares (unit + compound) in a 10x7 grid.

examples.question: How many numbers in this picture are in blue?
How many dogs in this picture?

notes.image_licensing: FSC-147 images are research-use licensed (MIT mirror on HuggingFace: isentropic/FSC147). Puzzle a Day images are from a personal blog and are bundled for internal hackathon evaluation only; do not redistribute.
suggested_hackathon_tasks: Compare prompting strategies: zero-shot ('how many X?') vs. structured ('count by sub-region').
Compare performance on real photos (FSC-147) vs. diagrams (geometry puzzles).
